exercises.title: Logical Operator Constructor
exercises.description: Build logical X and Z operators for surface codes.
tasks: Generate Z_L path connecting rough boundaries
Generate X_L path connecting smooth boundaries
Verify both operators have weight d
Show multiple equivalent Z_L operators (different columns)
Verify X_L and Z_L share exactly one qubit
hints: Weight = number of qubits in path

exercises.title: Stabilizer Commutation Checker
exercises.description: Verify logical operators commute with all stabilizers.
tasks: Enumerate all X and Z stabilizers
Check X_L commutes with each Z-stabilizer
Check Z_L commutes with each X-stabilizer
Count overlap qubits for each pair
Verify X_L anticommutes with Z_L
hints: Operators commute if they share even qubits
X_L overlaps Z-stabilizers on 0 or 2 qubits
Z_L overlaps X-stabilizers on 0 or 2 qubits
X_L and Z_L share exactly 1 qubit

exercises.title: State Preparation Simulator
tasks: Implement |0_L⟩ preparation protocol
Implement |+_L⟩ preparation protocol
Track stabilizer eigenvalues through preparation
Apply conditional corrections
Verify final state is in code space
hints: Apply X_L or Z_L based on logical measurement

exercises.title: Logical Measurement Analyzer
tasks: Simulate destructive Z_L measurement
Compute logical outcome from qubit outcomes
Extract error information from measurement pattern
Compare different Z_L paths (should give same result)
Handle measurement errors in logical outcome
hints: Measure all qubits in Z basis
Z_L = product of outcomes along any column
All columns should agree (up to stabilizers)
